articles.title: An Actor Learns About Doctors from a TV Show
articles.summary: Gerran Howell is an actor from Wales who plays a student doctor in a TV show called The Pitt. This show is about doctors and nurses working in a hospital in Pittsburgh, USA. Gerran says the show is very realistic because it has real doctors helping the actors. They watched videos and learned how to use medical tools to make it look real. He has a lot more respect for real doctors now because he sees how hard their job is. There is also a fun rumor that George Clooney, a famous actor, might have helped Gerran get the role. Gerran's character starts out scared but gets better at his job in the show. The show is made by people who want it to be accurate, so it teaches viewers about medicine too.

articles.title: Sudanese Band Plays Music for Peace During War
articles.summary: Aswat Almadina is a band from Sudan that plays music to inspire peace during a big war. The band members had to leave their home in Khartoum because it was dangerous, with dead bodies and destroyed buildings. They now live in different countries but still make music together. Their songs talk about social justice and help people feel hope. One fan said the music saved her life, showing how important it is. The band started in 2014 and mixes folk, pop, and jazz sounds. They became famous in Sudan and were the first band to tour the country. Even though there is war, they keep playing to bring people together and dream of peace.

articles.title: Scientists Measure Powerful Jets from Black Holes
articles.summary: Scientists have measured the power of jets coming from a black hole for the first time. They used a network of radio telescopes around the world to study a black hole called Cygnus X-1. This black hole is in a system with a big star. The jets are streams of energy that shoot out from the black hole. The scientists found that these jets are very powerful, with energy equal to 10,000 Suns. They also saw that the jets move at half the speed of light. The star's strong winds push the jets, making them dance around. This helps scientists understand how black holes affect space and galaxies. The research was done by a team from Curtin University and other places.

articles.title: Madonna Surprises Fans at Coachella with Sabrina Carpenter
articles.summary: Madonna surprised everyone at the Coachella music festival by joining Sabrina Carpenter on stage. They sang songs together, including Vogue and Like A Prayer, and a new song from Madonna's album. Madonna talked about how she performed at Coachella 20 years ago, making it a special moment for her. She joked that Sabrina is shorter than her, which was funny. Coachella is a big festival in California where many famous singers perform. This surprise made fans very happy and excited to see two pop stars together.

articles.title: How a Pop Star Helps Opera Grow
articles.summary: Opera is a type of music and theater that some people thought was old-fashioned, but now it's becoming more popular again. The Welsh National Opera says opera is more relevant today because of pop star Rosalía. She makes music that mixes opera with pop, and her songs are used in TikTok videos. This has brought new fans to opera. Also, people on social media like 'opera aesthetics,' which means dramatic and fancy styles. The WNO started 80 years ago with amateur singers like miners and teachers. They performed in a theater that is now a pub. Even though opera is getting more support, the WNO has faced money problems and had to cut back on tours. This shows how old art forms can change and stay exciting for new audiences.

articles.title: Oil Prices Drop as Iran Opens a Key Waterway
articles.summary: Oil prices went down a lot after Iran said it would keep the Strait of Hormuz open for ships during a ceasefire. The Strait of Hormuz is a narrow waterway where much of the world's oil travels. When it was closed, oil prices went up because less oil could get through. Now that Iran says it's open, prices fell from over $98 to $88 per barrel. This is good news for people who buy gas because lower oil prices can mean cheaper fuel. The U.S. president is happy about this, but some shipping groups are still checking to make sure it's safe. Markets around the world went up because of this news. The Strait had been closed since February when there were military strikes, causing oil to become very expensive. Even though Iran says it's open, some experts warn ships to be careful because there might still be dangers like mines.
